steps.label: What They Do
steps.body:
Avesha provides two related Kubernetes products:

- KubeSlice: secure, application-level connectivity that bridges multiple Kubernetes clusters across clouds and on-prem into a single virtual network
- Smart Autoscaler: workload-aware autoscaling that considers cost and performance signals

Key value: stage migrations off hyperscalers without rewriting application code.

steps.label: How They Run on Akamai
steps.body:
KubeSlice runs as a control plane on LKE plus operators in each connected cluster (LKE + EKS, GKE, AKS, on-prem). Microservices communicate across clusters as if they were local — zero code changes in the application.

Makes "lift one service at a time off AWS to LKE" a viable migration story instead of a rip-and-replace.

steps.label: When To Position
steps.body: Any customer mid-flight on a cloud migration who can't do a single-shot cutover. Reference: G&L Systemhaus uses KubeSlice to bridge media-processing microservices across on-prem and Akamai clusters for German broadcasters.
"Avesha KubeSlice = staged Kubernetes migration. Bridge clusters first, move workloads second, no code changes."

quiz.question: A customer wants to move from EKS to LKE but can't take a maintenance window. Which QCP unlocks staged migration?

options.explain: Neural Magic is AI inference acceleration — different problem.

options.text: Avesha KubeSlice — bridge EKS and LKE, then move workloads incrementally
options.explain: Exactly the use case. Connect clusters first, move services one at a time without code changes.

quiz.question: What's the key technical claim of KubeSlice?

options.explain: It runs on top of Kubernetes — doesn't replace it.

options.text: Application-level connectivity across clusters with no code changes
options.explain: Apps see services in another cluster as if they were local. No rewrite needed.
It bridges LKE with EKS, GKE, AKS, on-prem.
It works alongside the CNI, not as one.
